rows.title: A "The XML digital signature checker failed to initialize. (2038)" Error Occurs When Adding A Primary License In The License Manager 8 Administration Console.
rows.detailTitle: A "The XML digital signature checker failed to initialize. (2038)" Error Occurs When Adding A Primary License In The License Manager 8 Administration Console.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in A "The XML digital signature checker failed to initialize. (2038)" Error Occurs When Adding A Primary License In The License Manager 8 Administration Console. Product: Laserfiche Rio 8 KB: 1012767 Published: March 21, 2011 Last Updated: August 14, 2020 Summary When you attempt to add a primary license, you may receive the following error message: The XML digital signature checker failed to initialize. (2038) Cause This issue can occur when the License Manager service is running as Local System. In certain situations, an internal License Manager component is unable to access the user password for Local System. Workaround Create a new local user account on the License Manager server that has local administrator rights to the machine. Then configure the License Manager service to run under that user account instead of the Local System account. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: An "Error reading repository information, or the repository is misconfigured. [9025]" Error Occurs When Creating A Repository And The Laserfiche Server Is Running As A Local Machine Account.
rows.detailTitle: An "Error reading repository information, or the repository is misconfigured. [9025]" Error Occurs When Creating A Repository And The Laserfiche Server Is Running As A Local Machine Account.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in An "Error reading repository information, or the repository is misconfigured. [9025]" Error Occurs When Creating A Repository And The Laserfiche Server Is Running As A Local Machine Account. Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1013549 Published: September 22, 2014 Last Updated: September 22, 2014 Summary When creating a repository using Windows Authentication to authenticate to a Microsoft SQL Server instance and Laserfiche Server is running as a local machine account, you may receive the following error message: Error reading repository information, or the repository is misconfigured. [9025] The Windows Event Log will contain an entry with information similar to the following: There was an error attempting to execute SQL when creating or attaching a repository: [Microsoft][SQL Server Native Client 10.0][SQL Server]Login failed for user ''SampleDomain\MachineName$''.; SQL State: 28000; Native error code: 18456; Database server: SampleServerName; Database/Schema: SampleDatabaseName. Resolution Make sure that the Laserfiche Server service's logon account is a Windows user account that has sufficient permissions to the specified SQL Server instance. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

Product: Laserfiche 8, Laserfiche 9 KB: 1012169 Published: March 2, 2009 Last Updated: March 2, 2009 Summary When printing a document, your may receive the following error message: Unknown error. [6000:3221225477] Cause This error can occur if you select to print both images and text and the document does not contain any OCR text. Workaround On the Print dialog box, in the Print Type section, in the Print what drop-down list, select the Images option before clicking OK. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: A "Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Performing Records Management Actions on a Deeply-Nested Folder. 20 years ago A "Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Performing Records Management Actions on a Deeply-Nested Folder. July 8, 2005 | KB: 1001024 https://support.laserfiche.... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1001024/a-error-executing-sql-comm ...
rows.title: A "Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Performing Records Management Actions on a Deeply-Nested Folder.
rows.detailTitle: A "Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Performing Records Management Actions on a Deeply-Nested Folder.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in A "Error executing SQL command [7050]" Error Occurs When Performing Records Management Actions on a Deeply-Nested Folder. Product: Server (MSDE) 7, Server (MSSQL) 7 KB: 1001024 Published: July 8, 2005 Last Updated: July 8, 2005 Summary When you perform a records management action on a deeply nested folder, you may receive the following error message: AttemptedAction: Error executing SQL command Error executing SQL command [7050] In addition, the repository error.log file will contain the following error message: [7050] AttemptedAction failed (tocid=#): Maximum stored procedure, function, trigger, or view nesting level exceeded (limit 32). Cause Laserfiche Records Management utilizes nested triggers. Microsoft SQL Server contains a nesting limit of 32. For example, if you had a record series that contained 40 levels of nested folders, you would be unable to directly freeze the top level record series, as the limit prevents Laserfiche from recursively freezing all the necessary entries in the subsequent folder levels. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An "8000: Unknown error." Error Occurs When Logging in to a Repository. 12 years ago An "8000: Unknown error." Error Occurs When Logging in to a Repository. May 12, 2014 | KB: 1000451 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000451/an-quot-8000-unknown-error-quot-... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000451/an-8000-unknown-error-error
rows.title: An "8000: Unknown error." Error Occurs When Logging in to a Repository.
rows.detailTitle: An "8000: Unknown error." Error Occurs When Logging in to a Repository.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in An "8000: Unknown error." Error Occurs When Logging in to a Repository. Product: Laserfiche 6 KB: 1000451 Published: February 10, 2006 Last Updated: May 12, 2014 Summary When you log in to a repository, you may receive the following error message: 8000: Unknown error Cause There are a number of causes for this particular error and they are listed as follows: Stored procedures were not updated after upgrading the Laserfiche Server. See Resolution 1. One or more expansion databases have not been attached. See Resolution 2. Resolution 1 The Laserfiche Server uses stored procedures to access database data. This issue may arise when the stored procedures do not get updated after upgrading the Laserfiche Server. To update stored procedures using a VBScript: Click here to download the script. Save Version2.vbs file to a temporary directory. Double-click on the Version2.vbs file to begin running the script. From the first Laserfiche dialog box, type the name of the computer where the SQL Server resides. Click OK. Note: If you are using Laserfiche Team (Group), then you should append the following to the computer name: \Laserfiche. From the second Laserfiche dialog box, type in the name of the SQL database associated with the Laserfiche repository in question. Typically, the SQL database name is the same as the Laserfiche repository name. Click OK. Click "Yes" when asked to update. Restart the Laserfiche for Microsoft SQL Server service. This script resets the current stored procedure version number to "1." Restarting the Laserfiche Server will now automatically update the stored procedures. Note: In rare situations, when upgrading Laserfiche Server, running the Version2 VBScript and restarting the Laserfiche Server will not successfully update the stored procedures. This situation will only arise on a Laserfiche Server using the full version of Microsoft SQL Server. See the following workaround to manually populate the stored procedures. Workaround for Resolution 1 This workaround applies when upgrading to Laserfiche Server 6.11 or 6.12. This workaround should only be attempted if Resolution 1 fails to update the Laserfiche stored procedures. Manually populate the Laserfiche stored procedures into the appropriate SQL tables by running two SQL scripts in the Microsoft SQL Server Query Analyzer. Download the appropriate SQL scripts: Click here to download a zip file containing the necessary scripts. Extract the contents of 61x_SP_Update.zip to a temporary folder. Use "Wipe LFSPs.txt" to remove any Laserfiche stored procedures: Click Start and then select All Programs. Select Microsoft SQL Server and click Query Analyzer. Open the "Wipe LFSPs.txt" file you extracted from the previously downloaded zip file. Select and copy all of the text in the file. Paste the text into the Query window of the Query Analyzer. From the Query menu, click Execute. Use "611 update SPs.SQL" or "612 update SPs.SQL"to create the Laserfiche stored procedures: Click Start and then select All Programs. Select Microsoft SQL Server and click Query Analyzer. Perform one of the following: For Laserfiche Server 6.11: Use a text editor to open the "611 update SPs.SQL" file from the previously downloaded zip file. For Laserfiche Server 6.12: Use a text editor to open the "612 update SPs.SQL" file from the previously downloaded zip file. Select and copy all of the text in the file. Make sure the Query window is blank and paste the text into the Query window of the Query Analyzer. From the Query menu, click Execute. Manually reset the Laserfiche Stored Procedures version number and execute one final query: Click Start and then select All Programs. Select Microsoft SQL Server and click Query Analyzer. Perform one of the following: For Laserfiche Server 6.11 Make sure the Query window is blank and type the following in the Query window of the Query Analyzer: Update DBOptions set OptionValue=''660'' where OptionName=''Stored Procedure Version'' exec lfsp_grant_all_to_lf For Laserfiche Server 6.12 Make sure the Query window is blank and type the following in the Query window of the Query Analyzer: Update DBOptions set OptionValue=''665'' where OptionName=''Stored Procedure Version'' exec lfsp_grant_all_to_lf From the Query menu, click Execute. Resolution 2 This issue may also arise when an expansion database has not been attached. This section demonstrates two methods (VBScript and OSQL) through which you may attach expansion databases. To attach expansion databases using a VBScript file: Click here and here to download the necesssary script files. Run ListSqlDbs.vbs by double-clicking it. This script is designed to find SQL databases with similar names. From the first Laserfiche dialog box, type ServerName\Laserfiche. The term ServerName represents the name of the computer where the SQL server resides. Click OK. Note: If you upgraded Laserfiche Team (Group) to Laserfiche United (Standard\Enterprise), then you should only speci

Error Occurs When Loading Web Access 8. October 15, 2012 | KB: 1013069 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013069/a-quot-http-... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013069/a-http-error-503-the-servi ...
rows.title: A "HTTP Error 503. The service is unavailable." Error Occurs When Loading Web Access 8.
rows.detailTitle: A "HTTP Error 503. The service is unavailable." Error Occurs When Loading Web Access 8.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith A "HTTP Error 503. The service is unavailable." Error Occurs When Loading Web Access 8. Product: Web Access 8 KB: 1013069 Published: October 15, 2012 Last Updated: October 15, 2012 Summary When loading the Web Access 8 site, you may receive the following error message: HTTP Error 503. The service is unavailable. More Information This error can occur if the Web Access 8 application pool is running under invalid credentials. For example, if the application pool is running under a custom domain user account and the password is reset on that account. Open Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager and verify that there is a valid process model identity for the Web Access 8 application pool. By default, Web Access 8 runs under the WebAccess8AppPool application pool. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

August 2, 2004 | KB: 1000662 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000662/an-quot-error-cannot-create-file... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000662/an-error-cannot-create-fil ...
rows.title: An "Error cannot create file 7366." Error Occurs When Publishing.
rows.detailTitle: An "Error cannot create file 7366." Error Occurs When Publishing.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Error cannot create file 7366." Error Occurs When Publishing. Product: Plus 6 KB: 1000662 Published: August 2, 2004 Last Updated: August 2, 2004 Summary When you publishing a volume, you may receive the following error message: Error cannot create file 7366 Cause There are two possible causes for this error: There is insufficient disk space at the location to which you are trying to publish. See Resolution 1. You are logged in as a Windows user with insufficient rights to publish to the selected folder. See Resolution 2. Resolution 1 Free up or add more disk space. Resolution 2 Publish to a different folder. Make sure you are logged in as a user with sufficient Windows rights to write to the publishing folder. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

April 26, 2004 | KB: 1000532 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000532/an-quot-error-1355-quot-error https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000532/an-error-1355-error ...
rows.title: An "Error 1355." Error Occurs in the Application Event Viewer.
rows.detailTitle: An "Error 1355." Error Occurs in the Application Event Viewer.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An "Error 1355." Error Occurs in the Application Event Viewer. Product: Server (MSDE) 6, Server (MSSQL) 6 KB: 1000532 Published: April 26, 2004 Last Updated: April 26, 2004 Summary When the MSSQL$LASERFICHE service first starts, you may receive the following warning in the Microsoft Windows Application Event Viewer Log: The description for Event ID (19011) in Source (MSSQL$LASERFICHE) cannot be found. The local computer may not have the necessary registry information or message DLL files to display messages from a remote computer. The following information is part of the event: (SpnRegister): Error 1355. More Information LaserFiche performs normally, but this warning may regularly appear in the Windows Application Event Viewer. Microsoft states that this is not an error message, but a warning that SQL Server is using NTLM authentication instead of Kerberos. For more detailed information, please refer to Microsoft Knowledgebase article 303411. 303411 SQL Server 1355 Warning Errors are Informational Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 6. 8 months ago support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014577/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-weblink-11-update-6- WebLink 11 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014577/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 6.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 6.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 6. Product: WebLink 11 KB: 1014577 Published: October 14, 2025 Last Updated: October 14, 2025 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 6. The update includes the following changes for Laserfiche WebLink: Security update for third-party libraries. (614121) The update includes the following files for WebLink: \WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\bin\WebLink.dll (11.0.2510.5) \WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\bin\WLWebDesigner.dll (11.0.2510.5) \WebLink\WebLinkDesigner\bin\WLWebDesigner.dll (11.0.2510.5) \WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\javascript\resources\jquery-ui.min.js \WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\javascript\resources\jquery-ui.min.css Resolution Click the following link to download a zip file containing the update. KB1014577.zip On the computer where Laserfiche WebLink 11 is installed, extract and run Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 6 KB1014577.exe More Information To view installed updates: Open Control Panel, select Programs, and then select View Installed Updates. Verify if Laserfiche WebLink x64 11.0.2510.5 Update 6 KB:1014577 is in the list. Select the item and click Uninstall to remove the update. Related Links 1014439 WebLink 11 Updates 1014574 List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 5. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 5. Product: WebLink 11 KB: 1014574 Published: June 18, 2025 Last Updated: July 9, 2025 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 5. Update 5 addresses issues specific to Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 4. The update includes the following changes for Laserfiche WebLink: Accessibility updates to add alt-text for images, add labels to inputs and field types, add focus indicators for focusable elements, and increased contrast for certain visual elements. (590105, 593574) The Laserfiche Administration Console's Sessions node now shows WebLink 11 sessions with an application name of "WebLink 11." (499467) When viewing a document, using a quoted phrase in the "search in document" search bar now properly highlights the quoted search phrase in the context hit search results. (576978) The details pane now displays the correct document relationships for shortcuts. (582246) Viewing documents in Public Portal/WebLink may show a white grid across every page (589702) Custom search forms now properly display field column values when sorting by other columns. (590300) Subfolders are now included when exporting a folder. (590299) The update includes the following files for WebLink: \WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\bin\WebLinkControls.dll (11.0.2506.19) \WebLink\WebLink\Designer\bin\WebLinkControls.dll (11.0.2506.19) \WebLink\WebLink\Designer\bin\ar\WebLinkControls.resources.dll (11.0.2506.19) \WebLink\WebLink\Designer\bin\es\WebLinkControls.resources.dll (11.0.2506.19) \WebLink\WebLink\Designer\bin\fr\WebLinkControls.resources.dll (11.0.2506.19) \WebLink\WebLink\Designer\bin\it\WebLinkControls.resources.dll (11.0.2506.19) \WebLink\WebLink\Designer\bin\pt-BR\WebLinkControls.resources.dll (11.0.2506.19) \WebLink\WebLink\Designer\bin\zh-Hans\WebLinkControls.resources.dll (11.0.2506.19) Files\styles.css Resolution Click the following link to download a zip file containing the update. KB1014574.zip On the computer where Laserfiche WebLink 11 is installed, extract and run Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 5 KB1014574.exe More Information To View Installed Updates, open Control Panel, select Programs, and then select View Installed Updates. Verify if Laserfiche WebLink x64 11.0.2506.19 Update 5 KB:1014574 is in the list. Select the item and click Uninstall to remove the update. rows.snippet: Viewing documents in Public Portal/WebLink may show a white grid across every page 1 year ago support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014562/viewing-documents-in-public-portal-weblink-may-show-a-white-grid-across-every-page WebLink, Public Portal Summary When viewing documents in Laserfiche C... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014562/viewing-documents-in-public-porta ...
rows.title: Viewing documents in Public Portal/WebLink may show a white grid across every page
rows.detailTitle: Viewing documents in Public Portal/WebLink may show a white grid across every page
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Viewing documents in Public Portal/WebLink may show a white grid across every page Product: WebLink, Public Portal KB: 1014562 Published: February 28, 2025 Last Updated: June 18, 2025 Summary When viewing documents in Laserfiche Cloud Public Portal or self-hosted Laserfiche WebLink, the images may display improperly, with a white grid appearing over the pages. Cause The issue occurs when the web browser's zoom level is set below 100%. Resolution This issue is resolved in Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 5. Workaround There is a hotfix available for self-hosted Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 4 (11.0.2411.10) in the form of the following updated file: main.js (modified May 8, 2025) To update self-hosted Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 4 Click the following link to download a file update for Laserfiche WebLink 11 update 4 (11.0.2411.10): KB1014562.zip Create a backup of the original main.js located at C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\app\dist\doc-viewer\main.js. Place the backup in another location. Extract and place the updated main.js file into the C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\app\dist\doc-viewer folder, replacing the original file with the new file. Do not rename or leave the original file behind. Note: If you are not using the default "WebLink" web application and your "Web Files" folder is in a different location than the default "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\WebLink\WebLink\Web Files\" path, then replace the main.js file under the actual path used by your web application. Recycle the WebLinkAppPool in IIS Manager. Users must clear their web browser cache or perform a hard page refresh to see the fix. Workaround The workaround is to change browser zoom to 100%, and then use the Laserfiche WebLink/Public Portal document viewer's zoom controls to adjust the display as needed. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 3. 2 years ago support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014465/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-weblink-11-update-3- WebLink 11 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014465/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 3.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 3.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 3. Product: WebLink 11 KB: 1014465 Published: July 18, 2023 Last Updated: July 18, 2023 Summary This article details the list of changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 3. Update 3 addresses an issue specific to Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 2. Update 3 is a cumulative update and includes the changes from Update 2. The update includes the following changes for Laserfiche WebLink: Search forms and custom links are now able to be successfully added to the welcome page. (442833) The update includes the following files for WebLink: WebLink.dll (11.0.2307.136) WebLinkControls.dll (11.0.2307.136) WebLinkCommon.dll (11.0.2307.136) WLWebDesigner.dll (11.0.2307.136) icudt62.dll (62.1.0.2) icuin62.dll (62.1.0.0) icuuc62.dll (62.1.0.0) main.585cf12411d4d409fe71.js main.0c94a6b53befe1dcd7f9.js main.07d482e5d16b670c2bd2.js main.f7f8e06c46080e590055.js main.fcd74090645b76d4d2ac.js main.e896708f83035861b4dd.js styles.css Resolution Click the following link to download a zip file containing the update. KB1014465.zip On the computer where Laserfiche WebLink 11 is installed, extract and run Laserfiche WebLink Update 3 KB1014465.exe Important: This article was initially published on July 11, 2023, 11:12 AM PST and was subsequently unpublished later the same day at 5:14 PM PST. The patch included during that time frame contained an issue where it could only be installed over Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 1. This article has been republished on July 18, 2023, with an updated patch that corrects that installation issue. You cannot install the updated version of Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 3 over the previous version of Update 3. If you have previously downloaded and installed the patch from July 11, please uninstall the update, use the above link to redownload the update, and reinstall the update. More Information To check and uninstall the previous version of Update 3, use the Windows Control Panel to view and remove installed updates. To View Installed Updates, select Start, select Control Panel, select Programs, and then select View Installed Updates. Verify if Laserfiche WebLink x64 11.0.2306.122 Update 3 KB:1014465 is in the list. Select the item and click Uninstall to remove the update. Related Links 1014439 WebLink 11 Updates 1014438 List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 1. 1014456 List of Changes for Laserfiche WebLink 11 Update 2. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark
